titleStmt.title: SII 9.108: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 108. (A.R. No. 80 of 1904.) ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is very much damaged and dated Śaka 971 • • • • • • Sōma-grahaṇa. In Śaka 971 (current), Sarvadhārin, two lunar eclipses occurred, one in the month of Bhādrapada (= A.D. 1048 August 26, Friday), and the other in Phālguṇa (= A.D. 1049 February 20, Monday). There was no lunar eclipse in Śaka 971 expired. The record refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya king Traiḷōkyamalladēva. A Mahāsāmantādhipati (name lost), who was ruling over Paḻiyaṇḍa fourthousand, is stated to have made a gift of 12 mattars of land.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
gharmmarasmē raṇacayamatikramya tārāpathāntabhrāntassambhrāntastimi
bhavājñā bhūtyailōkō parara(ṁ)myōvaḷanabharamatikṣōbhitāṁbhōdhitōya
bhō tāṇḍavē bāhudaṇḍaḥ . svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrī
ra paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ śatyāśrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyā
rājyamuttarōttarābhivṛddhipravardhamānamācandrārkka
pañcamahāśabda mahāsāmantādhipati mahā
kabirudamukhadarppaṇanarasankarudra nārā
sāsavāri birudadīkṣāguru subhaṭaraṁjaka trai
ḷōkyamalladēvapādapaṁkajabhramaraṁ śrī
katanadiṁ paḻiyaṇḍa nālsāsira
tipāḷanegeydu sukhasaṁkathāvi
koṇḍu mūḻanadaṇḍano
noḷ sakavarṣa 971 ne
sōmagrahaṇadoḷ
rvvakaṁ māḍi sarvvanamasyavāgi
kōlalkariya keyi mattar 12
dātaṁ vāraṇāsi kurukṣētrameṁbī puṇnya brā
hmaṇarggubhayamukhigoṭṭa phalamaneyduvanidana ba puṇya
tīrtthaṁgaḷoḷsāsira kavileyaṁ sāsirvvarbrā ñcamahāpāta
kanakkuṁ . sāmānyōyaṁ dharmmasēturnṛpāṇāṁ yō bhavadbhiḥ . sarvvānētā
nbhāginaṟpārtthivēndrān bhūyō bhūyō yācatē rāmabhadra[ḥ* .] svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā
yō harēti vasundharāṁ . ṣaṣṭirvvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ . svaṁ dātuṁ
sumahacchakyaṁ duḥkhamanyatra pāḷanaṁ . dānādvā pāḷanādvēti dānāśrēyōnupāḷanaṁ .
Digital edition of SII 9.108 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
